data.body:
## Description

**What**: Add the sub-agent guard block to the remaining 9 target skills: review-protocol, resolve-conflicts, dev-onboarding, validate-work, retro, ui-discover (sub-agent dependent), and end-session, project-setup, design-onboarding (user-interaction dependent).
**Why**: Completes the guard coverage across all 18 target skills. The user-interaction variant uses the orchestrator signal phrase check instead of Agent tool availability.
**Scope**:
- IN: Add guard to 6 remaining sub-agent dependent skills, add user-interaction variant guard to 3 skills, make all 18 tests from Story 1 pass GREEN
- OUT: Guard pattern design (Story 1), documentation (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all 6 remaining sub-agent dependent skills contain the sub-agent guard block matching the pattern established in Story 1
  <- Satisfies: "Skills that require sub-agents use Agent tool availability as the detection method"
- When this story is complete, all 3 user-interaction dependent skills (end-session, project-setup, design-onboarding) contain a guard block that checks for the orchestrator signal phrase and exits with a clear error
  <- Satisfies: "Skills that require user interaction use the orchestrator signal phrase as the detection method"
- When this story is complete, the full test file from Story 1 passes GREEN for all 18 skills
  <- Satisfies: "The guard block follows a consistent pattern across all 18 skills"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Use the exact same guard block pattern from Story 1 for sub-agent dependent skills; use the user-interaction variant for the 3 interaction-dependent skills
